---
title: "AI Workshop Series"
subtitle: "Colab and Chill"
author: "NC State University Libraries"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../../config/slides/theme/ncsu.scss]
    incremental: false
    preview-links: auto
    chalkboard: true
    slide-number: true
---

## Welcome

Slides and materials live in this repository and the paired Colab notebook.

- Open the learner notebook in Colab
- Make your own copy before editing
- Follow along and experiment as we go

---

## Who is this workshop for?

- Learners who are new to Google Colab
- People who want a practical introduction to AI-assisted analysis workflows
- Anyone curious about how to prompt, inspect, and refine outputs rather than treating AI as magic

---

## What we will do

- Get comfortable with the Colab environment
- Practice writing clearer prompts
- Use AI to help inspect a small dataset
- Review results critically instead of accepting the first answer

---

## Core idea

AI can accelerate exploration, but it does not replace judgment.

Good workflow:

1. Start with a clear task
2. Ask for something inspectable
3. Check the result
4. Revise the prompt or code

---

## Colab workflow

1. Open the notebook
2. Copy it to Drive
3. Run cells top to bottom
4. Pause at each exercise before looking at the solution

---

## Prompting reminders

- Give the model context
- Ask for a specific output format
- Keep prompts small enough to verify
- Request explanations when you want to learn, not just copy

---

## Healthy skepticism

- Does the answer match the data you see?
- Are any assumptions unstated?
- Could you verify it with code?
- What would you change in the next prompt?

---

### Tell us how we did

[go.ncsu.edu/dss-workshop-eval](https://go.ncsu.edu/dss-workshop-eval)

<p class="thank-you-inline">THANK YOU</p>

::::::: {.hidden}
<script>
  document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => {
    const title = document.querySelector('.reveal .slides section .title, .reveal .slides section h1, .reveal .slides section h2');
    const workshop = title ? title.textContent.trim() : 'Workshop';
    document.body.setAttribute('data-workshop', workshop);
  });
</script>
::::::::
